The main reason cited by scientists for the current warming trend is an increase in the concentrations of greenhouse gases (GHGs) in the atmosphere, which naturally help keep the planet's temperature at a comfortable level. The amount of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere has increased by 35% since the dawn of the industrial age, according to the United Nations Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC).

The IPCC predicts that if GHG emissions continue to rise at their current rate, this century will see a further 3°C rise in the average world temperature. The consensus of the scientific community via the IPCC is that action must be taken to avert the worst impacts of climate change.
